volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 982 - Section 1.1070)

sections.label: Section 982
sections.original: ἱεραῖσιν ἐν σκηναῖσιν , οὗ θοινᾷ φίλους .
sections.translation: In the sacred tent , where he is feasting his friends .

sections.label: Section 983
sections.original: ἐπίσημον ὁ φόνος , καὶ τὸ δοῦλον ἀσθενές .
sections.translation: The murder will be too public , and slaves are poor support .

sections.label: Section 984
sections.original: ὤμοι , κακίζῃ · φέρε , σὺ νῦν βούλευέ τι .
sections.translation: Ah ! thou art turning coward . Devise some scheme thyself .

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἔχω γε δόλια καὶ δραστήρια .
sections.translation: Well , I too have subtle plans that cannot fail .

sections.label: Section 986
sections.original: ἀμφοῖν ἂν εἴην τοῖνδʼ ὑπηρέτης ἐγώ .
sections.translation: If both conditions they fulfil , I will assist thee .

sections.label: Section 987
sections.original: ἄκουε τοίνυν · οἶσθα γηγενῆ μάχην ;
sections.translation: Hearken then ; knowest thou the battle of the earth-born men ?

sections.label: Section 988
sections.original: οἶδʼ , ἣν Φλέγρᾳ Γίγαντες ἔστησαν θεοῖς .
sections.translation: Surely ; the fight at Phlegra waged by giants against the gods .

sections.label: Section 989
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα Γοργόνʼ ἔτεκε Γῆ , δεινὸν τέρας .
sections.translation: There Earth brought Gorgon forth , dreadful prodigy .

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: ἦ παισὶν αὑτῆς σύμμαχον , θεῶν πόνον ;
sections.translation: To aid her sons maybe , and cause the gods hard toil ?

sections.label: Section 991
sections.original: ναί · καί νιν ἔκτεινʼ ἡ Διὸς Παλλὰς θεά .
sections.translation: Yea , and Pallas , daughter of Zeus , slew the monster .

sections.label: Section 992
sections.original: ποῖόν τι μορφῆς σχῆμʼ ἔχουσαν ἀγρίας ;
sections.translation: What savage form had it assumed ?

sections.label: Section 993
sections.original: θώρακʼ ἐχίδνης περιβόλοις ὡπλισμένον .
sections.translation: A breast-plate of vipers fenced its body .

sections.label: Section 994
sections.original: ἆρʼ οὗτός ἐσθʼ ὁ μῦθος ὃν κλύω πάλαι ;
sections.translation: Is this the tale I heard in days of yore ?

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: ταύτης Ἀθάναν δέρος ἐπὶ στέρνοις ἔχειν .
sections.translation: That Athena wears its skin upon her corslet .

sections.label: Section 996
sections.original: ἣν αἰγίδʼ ὀνομάζουσι , Παλλάδος στολήν ;
sections.translation: Is it this that Pallas wears , called by men her aegis ?

sections.label: Section 997
sections.original: τόδʼ ἔσχεν ὄνομα θεῶν ὅτʼ ᾖξεν ἐς δόρυ .
sections.translation: This was the name it received , that day she came to do battle for the gods .

sections.label: Section 998
sections.original: τί δῆτα , θύγατερ , τοῦτο σοῖς ἐχθροῖς βλάβος ;
sections.translation: How , daughter , can this harm thy enemies ?

sections.label: Section 999
sections.original: Ἐριχθόνιον οἶσθʼ , ἢ — ; τί δʼ οὐ μέλλεις , γέρον ;
sections.translation: Hast heard of Erichthonius , or no ? of course thou hast .

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: ὃν πρῶτον ὑμῶν πρόγονον ἐξανῆκε γῆ ;
sections.translation: Him whom Earth produced , the founder of thy race ?

sections.label: Section 1001
sections.original: τούτῳ δίδωσι Παλλὰς ὄντι νεογόνῳ ⋯
sections.translation: To him whilst yet a babe did Pallas give —

sections.label: Section 1002
sections.original: τί χρῆμα ; μέλλον γάρ τι προσφέρεις ἔπος .
sections.translation: Ha ! what ? thou hast something yet to add ?

sections.label: Section 1003
sections.original: δισσοὺς σταλαγμοὺς αἵματος Γοργοῦς ἄπο .
sections.translation: Two drops of Gorgon ’ s blood .

sections.label: Section 1004
sections.original: ἰσχὺν ἔχοντας τίνα πρὸς ἀνθρώπου φύσιν ;
sections.translation: What power could they exert on the nature of a human creature ?

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: τὸν μὲν θανάσιμον , τὸν δʼ ἀκεσφόρον νόσων .
sections.translation: The one with death is fraught , the other cures disease .

sections.label: Section 1006
sections.original: ἐν τῷ καθάψασʼ ἀμφὶ παιδὶ σώματος ;
sections.translation: What held them when she tied them to the child ’ s body ?

sections.label: Section 1007
sections.original: χρυσοῖσι δεσμοῖς · ὁ δὲ δίδωσʼ ἐμῷ πατρί .
sections.translation: With links of gold she fastened them ; this to my sire did Erichthonius give .

sections.label: Section 1008
sections.original: κείνου δὲ κατθανόντος ἐς σὲ ἀφίκετο ;
sections.translation: And at his death it came to thee ?

sections.label: Section 1009
sections.original: ναί · κἀπὶ καρπῷ γʼ αὔτʼ ἐγὼ χερὸς φέρω .
sections.translation: Yea , and here at my wrist I wear it .

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: πῶς οὖν κέκρανται δίπτυχον δῶρον θεᾶς ;
sections.translation: How works the spell of this double gift of Pallas ?

sections.label: Section 1011
sections.original: κοίλης μὲν ὅστις φλεβὸς ἀπέσταξεν φόνῳ ⋯
sections.translation: Each drop of gore which trickled from the hollow vein —

sections.label: Section 1012
sections.original: τί τῷδε χρῆσθαι ; δύνασιν ἐκφέρει τίνα ;
sections.translation: What purpose does it serve ? what virtue does it carry ?

sections.label: Section 1013
sections.original: νόσους ἀπείργει καὶ τροφὰς ἔχει βίου .
sections.translation: Wards off disease , and nourishes man ’ s life .

sections.label: Section 1014
sections.original: ὁ δεύτερος δʼ ἀριθμὸς ὧν λέγεις τί δρᾷ ;
sections.translation: What doth that second drop effect , of which thou madest mention ?

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: κτείνει , δρακόντων ἰὸς ὢν τῶν Γοργόνος .
sections.translation: It kills , for it is venom from the Gorgon ’ s snakes .

sections.label: Section 1016
sections.original: ἐς ἓν δὲ κραθέντʼ αὐτὸν ἢ χωρὶς φορεῖς ;
sections.translation: Dost thou carry this charm mixed in one phial , or separate ?

sections.label: Section 1017
sections.original: χωρίς · κακῷ γὰρ ἐσθλὸν οὐ συμμείγνυται .
sections.translation: Separate ; for good is no companion for evil .

sections.label: Section 1018
sections.original: ὦ φιλτάτη παῖ , πάντʼ ἔχεις ὅσων σε δεῖ .
sections.translation: Daughter dear , thou art fully armed with all thou needest .

sections.label: Section 1019
sections.original: τούτῳ θανεῖται παῖς · σὺ δʼ ὁ κτείνων ἔσῃ .
sections.translation: By this must the boy die , and thou must do the deadly deed .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: ποῦ καὶ τί δράσας ; σὸν λέγειν , τολμᾶν δʼ ἐμόν .
sections.translation: How and where ? thine it is to speak , and mine to dare and do .

sections.label: Section 1021
sections.original: ἐν ταῖς Ἀθήναις , δῶμʼ ὅταν τοὐμὸν μόλῃ .
sections.translation: In Athens , when to my house he comes .

sections.label: Section 1022
sections.original: οὐκ εὖ τόδʼ εἶπας · καὶ σὺ γὰρ τοὐμὸν ψέγεις .
sections.translation: That is not wisely said ; I may object to thy plan as thou to mine .

sections.label: Section 1023
sections.original: πῶς ; ἆρʼ ὑπείδου τοῦθʼ ὃ κἄμʼ ἐσέρχεται ;
sections.translation: How so ? Hast thou the same mistrust that I experience ?

sections.label: Section 1024
sections.original: σὺ παῖδα δόξεις διολέσαι , κεἰ μὴ κτενεῖς .
sections.translation: Thou wilt get the credit of his death , although thou slay him not .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: ὀρθῶς · φθονεῖν γάρ φασι μητρυιὰς τέκνοις .
sections.translation: True ; men say stepdames are jealous of their husband ’ s children .

sections.label: Section 1026
sections.original: αὐτοῦ νυν αὐτὸν κτεῖνʼ , ἵνʼ ἀρνήσῃ φόνους .
sections.translation: Kill him here then , that so thou mayst deny the murder .

sections.label: Section 1027
sections.original: προλάζυμαι γοῦν τῷ χρόνῳ τῆς ἡδονῆς .
sections.translation: Well , thus I do anticipate the pleasure .

sections.label: Section 1028
sections.original: καὶ σόν γε λήσεις πόσιν ἅ σε σπεύδει λαθεῖν .
sections.translation: Yea , and thou wilt from thy husband keep the very secret he would keep from thee .

sections.label: Section 1029
sections.original: οἶσθʼ οὖν ὃ δρᾶσον ; χειρὸς ἐξ ἐμῆς λαβὼν
sections.translation: Dost know then what to do ? Take from my arm

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: χρύσωμʼ Ἀθάνας τόδε , παλαιὸν ὄργανον ,
sections.translation: this golden bracelet , Athena ’ s gift , some ancient craftsman ’ s work , and seek the spot where my lord is offering secret sacrifice ; then when their feasting is o ’ er and they are about to pour drink-offering to the gods , take this phial in thy robe and pour it into the young man ’ s goblet ;

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ἰδίᾳ δέ , μή τι πᾶσι , χωρίσας ποτὸν
sections.translation: not for all , but for him alone , providing a separate draught , who thinks to lord it o ’ er my house . And if once it pass his lips , never shall he come to glorious Athens , but here abide , of life bereft .

sections.label: Section 1039
sections.original: σὺ μέν νυν εἴσω προξένων μέθες πόδα ·
sections.translation: Go thou within the house of our public hosts ;

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δʼ ἐφʼ ᾧ τετάγμεθʼ ἐκπονήσομεν .
sections.translation: I the while will set about my appointed task . On ! aged foot , grow young again in action , for all that time saith no to thee . Go , aid thy mistress against her enemy , help slay and drag him from her house .

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: τὴν δʼ εὐσέβειαν εὐτυχοῦσι μὲν καλὸν
sections.translation: ’ Tis well to honour piety in the hour of fortune , but when thou wouldst harm thy foe , no law doth block thy path .

sections.label: Section 1.1048
sections.original: Εἰνοδία θύγατερ Δάματρος , ἃ τῶν
sections.translation: Daughter of Demeter , goddess of highways , queen as thou art of haunting powers of darkness ,

sections.label: Section 1.1050
sections.original: καὶ μεθαμερίων , ὅδωσον
sections.translation: oh ! guide as well the hand that fills by day a cup of death , against those to whom my revered mistress is sending a philtre of the gore that dripped from hellish Gorgon ’ s severed head ,

sections.label: Section 1.1055
sections.original: σταλαγμῶν
sections.translation: yea , ’ gainst him who would obtrude upon the halls of the Erechthidae . Never may alien , from alien stock , lord it o ’ er my city ,

sections.label: Section 1.1060
sections.original: πλὴν τῶν εὐγενετᾶν Ἐρεχθειδᾶν .
sections.translation: no ! none save noble Erechtheus ’ sons !

sections.label: Section 1.1061
sections.original: εἰ δʼ ἀτελὴς θάνατος σπουδαί τε δεσποί-
sections.translation: For if this deadly deed and my lady ’ s aims pass unfulfilled , and the right moment for her daring go by , and with it the hope which now sustains her , either will she seize the whetted knife

sections.label: Section 1.1065
sections.original: ψει βρόχον ἀμφὶ δειρήν ,
sections.translation: or fasten the noose about her neck , and by ending one sorrow by another will go down to other phases of existence . For never will that daughter of a noble line ,

sections.label: Section 1.1070
sections.original: ἄρχοντας ἀλλοδαποὺς
sections.translation: while life is hers , endure within the sunshine of her eyes the sight of alien rulers in her halls .
